results.title: Optimizing Visual Generative Models via Distribution-wise Rewards
results.content:
Our contributions are summarized as follows:

We analyze the limitations of reinforcement learning with sample-wise reward functions, showing that they are susceptible to reward hacking, which degrades distributional fidelity and introduces artifacts while reducing diversity.

We propose a RL framework with distribution-wise rewards by the subset-replace strategy. This provides a robust alternative to conventional sample-wise rewards, which are vulnerable to reward hacking. Through extensive experiments, we derive an effective and optimal training recipe that reduces the FID-50K of SiT from 8.30 to 5.77 and the FDDINOv2\text{FD}\_{\text{DINOv2}} score from 230.39 to 164.88 without requiring additional training data or architectural modifications. [...] statistical metrics on a huge set of images (e.g., 50K samples for FID), incurring significant computational cost. Besides, such distribution-wise metrics can’t provide reward signals for each individual denoising trajectories that is necessary for RL training. Moreover, we observed that performance improvements from RL fine-tuning in a SDE-based stochastic environment (Fan et al., 2023; Liu et al., 2025; He et al., 2025; Wang and Yu, 2025) for exploration do not fully translate to the faster, ODE-based deterministic sampling used during inference process. This discrepancy highlights a significant train-inference inconsistency and motivates the search for alternative optimization methods that avoids the performance gap between SDE-based training and ODE-based inference. [...] In this work, we propose distribution-wise reward for RL training. Specifically, we use a novel subset-replace strategy to obtain dense distribution-wise reward signals at a low compute cost. First, we generate a reference set of images and compute its FID against the target distribution as a starting point. During rollouts, a small subset of this reference set is replaced by newly generated samples, and the FID of the updated set is used as a dense reward signal.

results.title: Tremendous Alternatives: Custom Incentive Platforms for Global Teams - Online Rewards
results.content:
### Key Feature #1 – Build incentive programs around your specific KPIs, not the limitations of a one-size-fits-all platform. Never compromise your business logic or settle for low-impact results.

Unlike Tremendous and other API-focused gift card platforms that offer limited customization, Online Rewards provides a Modular Program Architecture that lets you build flexible incentive programs using interchangeable modules for sales spiffs, milestones, quizzes, and multi-brand deployments. This isn’t just about distributing rewards, it’s about creating sophisticated behavior-based programs that drive specific business outcomes. [...] The Enterprise Integration Framework addresses another critical pain point: the limitations of inflexible SaaS solutions that don’t integrate with existing enterprise systems. Through API-driven connections to HRIS, CRM, payroll, and learning systems with real-time triggers and secure endpoints, Online Rewards seamlessly connects with your existing tech stack. This eliminates the fragmented engagement platforms requiring multiple vendors for different reward components that plague enterprises using basic alternatives.

### Key Feature #2 – Global, tax-optimized fulfillment with transparent bill-on-redemption pricing, helping you avoid compliance risks, budget overruns, and scaling headaches that come with one-size-fits-all solutions. [...] No more piecing together third-party tax services, struggling with manual compliance checks, or dealing with delayed fulfillment. With Online Rewards, you get a turnkey solution that scales with your business — confidently, compliantly, and globally.

## 2. Bonusly

results.title: Understanding: Rewards Distribution
results.content:
max_rewards.png

## Reward Distribution

There are a number of ways you can distribute rewards: randomly-chosen winning moments each hour, each day or within a custom time window; classic odds-based distribution such as odds of 1 in 100; Always Win reward which can be given away in addition to an odds or time-based reward, and, finally, 'Consolation Reward (used to be called Everyone Wins)' for those who weren't lucky enough to receive a prize using any of the other methods.

Let’s look at the options in detail:

### Hourly [...] Example:   
Reward 1: Unlimited number of Always Win rewards  
Reward 2: Odds based reward where 1 in 100 win this reward  
  
All entrant who successfully enter the Experience will receive the Always Win reward. In addition to that, everyone will have a 1 in 100 chance to win the additional odds-based reward.

### Consolation Reward [...] ### Always Win

 Always Win is a unique type of a reward because it is the only reward that is always granted and gives user additional chance to win a secondary prize (if available) based on time or odds
 It cannot be combined with Consolation Rewards
 You won’t be asked to define any other criteria, but if you have limited number of rewards to distribute, don’t forget to set the max rewards at the beginning
 You can also decide to meter the reward, which is a setting that will help limit the number of rewards in a day or week if you want to spread the prize distribution over period of time

results.title: [PDF] Distributional Reward Decomposition for Reinforcement Learning
results.content:
The architecture is rather limited due to requiring prior knowledge of how to split into sub-states.
Grimm and Singh  propose a more general method for reward decomposition via maximizing ∗Contributed during internship at Microsoft Research.
33rd Conference on Neural Information Processing Systems (NeurIPS 2019), Vancouver, Canada.
disentanglement between sub-rewards. In their work, an explicit reward decomposition is learned via maximizing the disentanglement of two sub-rewards estimated with action-value functions. [...] To address this problem, we utilize an idea similar to universal value function approximation (UVFA) (Schaul et al. ). The key idea is to take one-hot embedding as an additional in-put to condition the categorical distribution function, and apply the element-wise multiplication ψ ⊙φ, to force interaction between state features and the one-hot embedding feature: Fi(x, a) = Fθi(ψ(x) ⊙φ(ei))a, (10) where ei denotes the one-hot embedding where the i-th element is one and φ denotes one-layer non-linear neural network that is updated by backpropagation during training.
In this way, the agent explicitly learns different distribution functions for different channels. The complete network architecture is shown in Figure 1(a). [...] Our work also relates to Horde (Sutton et al. ). The Horde architecture consists of a large number of ‘sub-agents’ that learn in parallel via off-policy learning. Each demon trains a separate general value function (GVF) based on its own policy and pseudo-reward function. A pseudo-reward can be any feature-based signal that encodes useful information. The Horde architecture is focused on building up general knowledge about the world, encoded via a large number of GVFs. UVFA (Schaul et al. ) extends Horde along a different direction that enables value function generalizing across different goals. Our method focuses on learning implicit reward decomposition in order to more efﬁciently learn a control policy.

results.title: Rewards
results.content:
Via App

How To - Track Rewards

1. Login to your account in the app and click on 'More' in the bottom right hand corner of the app.

How To - Track Rewards

2. Click on "MORE Rewards".

How To - Track Rewards

3. Scroll down and tap "Rewards History".

How To - Track Rewards

4. Toggle between the different reward categories at the top of the screen and view your earning and redemption history.

Via Web

1. Log into your account and tap Rewards in the header navigation. Then select Manage MORE Rewards.

2. Click on "View Rewards".

How To - Track Rewards

3. Scroll down and tap "Rewards History".

How To - Track Rewards

4. Toggle between the different reward categories at the top of the screen and view your earning and redemption history.

How To - Track Rewards
